content: The study-family rollout is complete. All 102 primary dataset rows now have study_family, component_of_dataset_id, count_as_independent_cohort, and overlap_notes populated. Current countability is 81 TRUE, 5 FALSE, and 16 Unclear across 85 distinct study/source families. The Datasets detail panel exposes these fields, global search can find study/trial family names, and the Curation Methods page documents the rollout with links to review artifacts.

TRUE means countable based on current evidence, not proof of no hidden donor overlap. The 16 Unclear rows still need donor/sample reconciliation before cohort counts can be treated as de-duplicated. Some family labels group conceptually related studies without proving shared donors, especially therapeutic trials, PROTECT/RISK-related rows, and functional-genomics rows.
Resolve the 16 Unclear countability rows, starting with PROTECT, RISK, UNITI, SP140, NatCommun2023 multimodal rows, and GEMINI/IFX.
Add a cohort-counting QA report showing total rows, unique study families, TRUE/FALSE/Unclear counts, component rows, invalid parent IDs, and missing overlap notes.
Improve user-facing family search affordance with a compact note or examples near the Dataset Explorer search box, without adding a large 85-family dropdown.
